name: GHZ State
long_description: The GHZ state generalises the Bell state to three (or more) qubits. It exhibits genuine multipartite entanglement: no qubit is separable from the others. It violates the Mermin inequality, ruling out local hidden variable theories. Measuring any one qubit immediately collapses all others to a definite state.

references.title: Greenberger, Horne, Zeilinger (1989). Going Beyond Bell's Theorem

author: OpenQC Community
